what_it_does: Creates public.clients — the client registry holding each remote-MCP client's full policy in one place: id, label, allowed_tools (jsonb of {tool: {remote_posture, allowed ops, scope}}), scopes[], approval_mode, channels[], default posture, status, timestamps. Adds table/column comments documenting the fail-closed posture rule, sets grants (service_role/postgres ALL, anon/authenticated SELECT), and seeds the first row, 'claude-design', with a concrete allowed_tools map and scopes/channels.
observations: allowed_tools jsonb encodes per-tool {remote_posture, allowed (op allow-list or null), scope}; column comment defines posture vocabulary safe|design|consent|hazard|locked
Fail-closed rule stated in comment: a tool absent, or with posture not in {safe,design}, is NOT exposed
Header comment says this is the Supabase home of what was remote_exposure.json — config folded into the company DB
Both the Edge Function gate and the Python gateway resolve policy from here at call time (registry-is-truth; gateway owns no private allow-list)

Seeded claude-design row: ~14 safe read tools (some with op allow-lists like corpus/sessions/channels/marks/operator) plus two design-posture write tools (cc_channel:send, claude_design:[ingest_bundle,submit_seed,walk_decision])
scopes seeded as company:design:read + company:design:write; channels ['design-pipeline']; approval_mode 'operator-gate'
Seed uses on conflict (id) do nothing (idempotent insert)
standouts: registry-is-truth: a single DB row is the authoritative policy resolved by both the Edge gate and the Python gateway
The concrete claude-design allow-list with per-tool op restrictions and posture/scope tags
Fail-closed exposure rule encoded as the absence/posture default
conventions: Self-describing table/column comments documenting the policy semantics
Same grant pattern as 0001 (service_role ALL, read roles SELECT)
concerns: posture, approval_mode, status, scope values are free text/jsonb enforced only by comment, not DB CHECK constraints
anon SELECT exposes the client policy/allow-list to the public anon key
updated_at has a default but no trigger shown here to maintain it on UPDATE
notes: The single-source client registry: one row per remote-MCP client carrying its tool/scope/channel/approval policy, seeded with the claude-design allow-list folded in from remote_exposure.json.
questions: Where are posture/scope values validated if not by DB constraints — only in the gateway code?
What maintains updated_at on UPDATE (a trigger in a later migration, or app code)?
Will the 'shared posture registry the rows reference' mentioned in the comment arrive in a later migration?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — stated purpose (one place a client's remote-MCP policy lives, resolved at call time) is what the table + seed implement.
apparent_themes: config-into-company fold (json file becomes DB rows)
intention_signals: Centralises remote-MCP authorization into one auditable, queryable DB row per client so policy is data the gate/gateway resolve, not code they each carry — and so adding a client is adding a row.
novelty: Folding a former JSON exposure config into a DB-resident registry with per-tool posture+op+scope as jsonb, consumed by two independent enforcers.
contribution: Brings the client-policy data model and the seeded claude-design policy — the authoritative allowed_tools/scopes/channels that the gateway and Edge gate read to decide what a remote client may call.
summary_for_embedding: SQL migration creating public.clients, the remote-MCP client registry (allowed_tools jsonb, scopes, channels, approval_mode, posture) with a fail-closed exposure rule, seeded with the claude-design client policy.
intention_for_embedding: To make each remote client's tool/scope/channel policy a single authoritative DB row resolved at call time by both the Edge gate and the Python gateway.
